xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Non-redundant pathway selection after GSEA via leading-edge CMI

Source: R/run_gsea_redundancy_selection.R
run_gsea_redundancy_selection.Rd

Bridges adaptive GSEA (run_enrichment) and information- theoretic redundancy selection (run_redundancy_selection) by using each pathway's leading edge,the genes driving the enrichment score peak,as the unit of comparison rather than full annotated pathway membership.

run_gsea_redundancy_selection(
  gsea_results,
  gene_stats,
  alpha = 0.05,
  min_gain = NULL,
  fraction = 0.15,
  n_perm_it = 500L,
  n_perm_gain = 200L,
  max_pathways = 20L,
  max_union_fraction = 0.3,
  min_leading_edge = 3L,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

gsea_results

Named list of results from run_enrichment(..., method = "gsea"). Names are used as pathway identifiers and must be unique.

gene_stats

Named numeric vector of per-gene scores used in the original GSEA run (e.g. log fold-change, t-statistic). Names are gene symbols. Used to define the universe.

alpha

Numeric. Significance threshold for filtering gsea_results. Default 0.05.

min_gain

NULL (default, auto-select) or positive numeric. Passed to run_redundancy_selection.

fraction

Numeric in (0, 1). Relevance fraction for auto min_gain. Default 0.15. Passed to run_redundancy_selection.

n_perm_it

Integer. Permutations for IT enrichment null. Default 500L.

n_perm_gain

Integer. Permutations for auto min_gain noise floor. Default 200L.

max_pathways

Integer. Hard cap on selected pathways. Default 20L.

max_union_fraction

Numeric in (0, 1). Warn if the leading-edge union exceeds this fraction of the universe. Default 0.30.

min_leading_edge

Integer. Minimum leading edge size to include a pathway. Pathways with very small leading edges (e.g. 1-2 genes) are dropped as MI estimation is unreliable. Default 3L.

seed

Optional integer passed to set.seed.

Value

A data frame with one row per selected pathway in selection order. Inherits all columns from run_redundancy_selection output, plus:

p_value

GSEA p-value from gsea_results.

enrichment_score

Signed enrichment score.

leading_edge_size

Number of genes in the leading edge.

full_set_size

Full annotated pathway size (overlap with universe), for comparison with leading_edge_size.

Attributes:

min_gain

Threshold used (auto or supplied).

union_size

Size of the leading-edge union gene list.

universe_size

Size of the universe.

union_fraction

Union as fraction of universe.

n_sig

Number of significant pathways entering selection.

n_dropped_small_le

Pathways dropped for small leading edge.

Details

This addresses a limitation of applying redundancy selection directly to annotated gene sets after GSEA: two pathways may share 60\ annotated members yet have completely non-overlapping leading edges, meaning they are driven by independent biology despite superficial overlap. Conversely, pathways with modest annotation overlap may share their entire leading edge, making them functionally redundant.

Algorithm

  1. Filter gsea_results to pathways with p_value <= alpha.

  2. Extract the leading_edge from each significant result.

  3. Construct a background gene list as the union of all significant leading edges. This represents the set of genes that drove enrichment in at least one significant pathway. A warning is emitted if this union exceeds max_union_fraction of the universe (default 0.30), as MI becomes less discriminating when the gene list covers a large fraction of the background.

  4. Score each leading edge by mutual information with the union gene list via run_info_enrichment. MI naturally penalises large leading edges that cover much of the union (low surprise) and rewards small, concentrated ones (high surprise).

  5. Run greedy CMI selection via run_redundancy_selection using the leading edges as gene sets and the union as the query list.

  6. Attach GSEA diagnostics (p-value, enrichment score, leading edge size) to the selection output.

Why leading edges and not full pathway membership

The leading edge is the subset of pathway genes that actually drove the enrichment score, genes ranked before the running-sum peak. Full pathway membership includes many genes with weak or absent signal. Using leading edges means redundancy is assessed on the biologically active subset, not on database annotation boundaries which are often arbitrary.

Connection to run_redundancy_selection

This function is a wrapper that constructs appropriate inputs for run_redundancy_selection. All min_gain, fraction, and max_pathways arguments are passed through unchanged. The key difference from calling run_redundancy_selection directly is the automatic construction of gene_list from leading edges and the attachment of GSEA diagnostics to the output.

See also

run_enrichment for GSEA with adaptive permutation testing, run_redundancy_selection for the underlying CMI selection, run_info_enrichment for marginal IT enrichment scores, plot_gains to visualise the selection profile.

Examples

set.seed(1)
universe   <- paste0("G", 1:1000)
gene_stats <- setNames(rnorm(1000), universe)
gene_stats[universe[1:70]]    <- gene_stats[universe[1:70]]    + 4
gene_stats[universe[500:530]] <- gene_stats[universe[500:530]] + 3

# Three pathway groups:
#   A1/A2/A3 - redundant, driven by top-ranked genes
#   B1 - independent signal, different gene region
#   N1 - noise
gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A1 = universe[1:60],
  pathway_A2 = c(universe[1:55], universe[61:65]),
  pathway_A3 = c(universe[5:60], universe[66:70]),
  pathway_B1 = universe[500:530],
  pathway_N1 = sample(universe[200:400], 40)
)

# Run GSEA on each pathway
gsea_results <- lapply(gene_sets, function(gs) {
  run_enrichment(
    gene_list  = gs,
    gene_stats = gene_stats,
    method     = "gsea",
    n_perm     = 500L,
    seed       = 1
  )
})
names(gsea_results) <- names(gene_sets)

# Select non-redundant pathways based on leading edges
sel <- run_gsea_redundancy_selection(
  gsea_results = gsea_results,
  gene_stats   = gene_stats,
  alpha        = 0.05,
  seed         = 1
)
#> 4 / 5 pathways significant at alpha = 0.05.
#> Leading-edge union: 95 genes (9.5% of universe).
#> Computing IT enrichment scores on leading edges...
#> Running CMI redundancy selection on leading edges...
#> Auto-selected min_gain = 0.03417 bits (noise floor = 0.00409, relevance threshold = 0.03417 [15% of leading MI]).
print(sel)
#>            step    pathway set_size marginal_mi conditional_mi redundancy_ratio
#> pathway_A1    1 pathway_A1       58   0.2278304     0.22783036        1.0000000
#> pathway_B1    2 pathway_B1       28   0.1011582     0.15219695        1.5045435
#> pathway_A2    3 pathway_A2       58   0.2278304     0.03584464        0.1573304
#> pathway_A3    4 pathway_A3       58   0.2278304     0.03779196        0.1658776
#>            cumulative_bits     p_value enrichment_score leading_edge_size
#> pathway_A1       0.2278304 0.001996008        0.9484277                58
#> pathway_B1       0.3800273 0.001996008        0.8554779                28
#> pathway_A2       0.4158719 0.001996008        0.9485106                58
#> pathway_A3       0.4536639 0.001996008        0.9400472                58
#>            full_set_size
#> pathway_A1            60
#> pathway_B1            31
#> pathway_A2            60
#> pathway_A3            61
